entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing and role of Artificial Intelligence
entry.content:
The manufacturing industry has undergone a significant transformation, progressing from mechanical to electrical to more abstract concepts. With advancements in technologies such as artificial intelligence, we are witnessing the emergence of intelligent, self-operating systems. While sophisticated technologies like robotics, machine learning, and the Internet of Things are not entirely new in manufacturing, their integration into production lines, both in discrete and process manufacturing, has been steadily growing.

Thanks to developments in Generative AI, organizations are vying to become intelligent enterprises at a breakneck pace. Petabytes of data and millions of smart sensors have made it challenging for big OEMs to use the data meaningfully so that they can act on the data when necessary and gain insights.

SAP Digital Manufacturing solution offers a range of impressive features and functionalities for manufacturing execution and analytics. It facilitates smart factory use cases where intelligent technologies can drive organizations toward greater autonomy, resilience, and sustainability.

SAP's strategic integration of embedded AI into business processes is revolutionizing operations for many organizations that have been deterred by the complexities of developing, maintaining, and deploying AI applications.

One particularly promising application of AI in manufacturing is visual inspection for quality control. Numerous organizations across various industries, including automotive, consumer products, food and beverage, and high-tech manufacturing, are already harnessing the power of machine learning for this purpose. However, generative AI and computer vision hold even greater potential for identifying defects and logging non-conformances in non-traditional and highly customized manufacturing processes.

Another area where many organizations face challenges is manufacturing analytics. The demand for real-time access to relevant manufacturing data on a unified platform is driving the need for innovative solutions. SAP Digital Manufacturing offers a comprehensive solution for deploying AI and ML models on both structured and unstructured data. This foundation enables essential analytics for operational insights and live monitoring, which are crucial for every manufacturing organization.

This architecture empowers users to visualize critical trends and metrics in a single dashboard, eliminating the need to log into multiple systems. The no-code application build platform within SAP Digital Manufacturing allows users to create custom dashboards and leverage SAP Analytics Cloud for embedded analytics. Additionally, features like query designer facilitate the easy creation of key performance indicators (KPIs) based on specific logic.

Line reporting, on the other hand, provides real-time visibility into shop floor processes through Production Operator Dashboards (PODs). Line Monitor PODs enable shop floor schedulers to visualize the execution of shop floor control orders (SFCs) in real time and respond to alerts, scrap, or quality-related non-conformances.

Manufacturing automation is becoming increasingly sophisticated due to technological advancements. This automation has revolutionized the production of goods, boosting efficiency, quality, and innovation. By minimizing human error, automation contributes to higher-quality products and lower defect rates. SAP Digital Manufacturing provides a powerful automation platform, including manufacturing APIs and services, to automate manufacturing processes and integrate different plant systems seamlessly, fostering a connected manufacturing environment.

SAP Digital Manufacturing offers flexible extension options, including "In-App" and "Side-by-side" extension features, enabling seamless integration with other systems and data harmonization. The solution also supports the integration of external machine learning models developed and trained outside of Digital Manufacturing.

Custom-built business services or APIs can be easily integrated into the standard production process. Process extensions may require custom integration flows within the SAP BTP-based integration suite, incorporating various BAPIs, OData, and REST services to connect with digital manufacturing.

The potential of AI in manufacturing and supply chains is immense. Use cases include error identification and root cause analysis, simulation, and outlier detection, and the transformative "actions-to-insights" approach. By integrating AI with process automation and other technologies, organizations can effectively leverage "insights-to-action" capabilities.

To learn more about how SAP Digital Manufacturing will revolutionize your operations, order your copy today. 

Authors : Rahul Kumar (rahul.kumar04@sap.com) , Rohit Dwivedi (r.dwivedi@sap.com) 

https://www.sap-press.com/introducing-sap-digital-manufacturing_5948/

entry.title: Enhance Task Approvals with the Power of AI
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced corporate environment, delays in task approvals can significantly hamper productivity, create bottlenecks, and escalate costs. When approval processes are slow, employees often face frustration, morale dips, and businesses run the risk of missing critical opportunities or failing to meet regulatory compliance deadlines. Particularly in industries that thrive on innovation, these delays can obstruct competitiveness and diminish the organization’s ability to respond swiftly to market changes.

To address these challenges, Task Center is launching a revolutionary AI-powered feature called Intelligent Recommendations. This functionality leverages artificial intelligence to streamline and accelerate task approval processes by analyzing key attributes relevant to specific business cases and generating predictive models for task approvals.

The initial version of Intelligent Recommendations focuses on tasks created using SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)—a cloud-based workflow management solution—with plans to expand compatibility with other providers in future updates.

Prerequisites:

To use the Intelligent Recommendations feature, the SBPA service must be instantiated within the tenant, as the AI engine resides there. This initial implementation only supports process automation through SBPA, utilizing data that can be accessed from the SBPA engine.

User Perspectives on Intelligent Recommendations

Business User:

From the business user’s perspective, Intelligent Recommendations operates on a per-task-type level. A critical element of this functionality is the Confidence Level, which determines the AI’s confidence in automatically approving tasks based on the specified criteria.

Task Type Selection:
Begin by selecting the desired task type and clicking the "Go" button. This action will display the confidence level field, where approval conditions can be defined.    

Setting Confidence Levels:
Users can specify a confidence level for automatic task approvals. If the confidence level falls below 70%, the task will not qualify for automatic approval. Available options for defining the confidence level include: Less than,Equal to, Between and Greater than. 

                     

Note:
Clicking the "Go" button is essential when setting up the confidence level, as this ensures the changes are applied correctly and helps optimize system performance. 

 

Sorting by Confidence Levels:

Once the confidence level is set, it becomes a sortable column within the task table. This enables users to prioritize tasks based on their confidence level, facilitating a more organized and efficient approval process

Administrator View: Managing Intelligent Recommendations

Administration Cockpit:

Administrators have access to the Intelligent Recommendations tab within the Task Center’s administration cockpit. This section provides a comprehensive overview of all models, allowing administrators to create, manage, and adjust AI-powered task approval models as business requirements evolve.

Steps to Create a Model:

  1. Accessing the Model Creation Feature:
    To create a new model, Go to Administration cockpit  and Select Intelligent Reccomendations tab. click on the "Create Model" button.                                   

    Please note that only authorized administrators or key users can perform this action, so ensure the appropriate user roles are in place.

  2. Filling in the Model Details:
    Upon clicking "Create Model," a pop-up window will appear with the following fields: 
      • Task Provider: At present, only SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) is available as a task provider.
      • Task Type: Select the appropriate task type from the available dropdownlist.
      • Model Name: Provide a unique name for the model, which will be used to identify it in the list of models.
      • Description: Enter a description that reflects the model's purpose from administrative point of view.                                                      
  3. Selecting Task Type Attributes:
    Administrators can choose from a list of available task attributes:
    • Categorical Attributes: These are static attributes that remain consistent ver time.
    • Numerical Attributes: Dynamic attributes that may change, reflecting variations in the business environment.
  4. Completing the Model Creation:
    After configuring the model, click "Create". The newly created model will appear in the list with its initial status as either Active or In Training.                    
  5. Status and Activation:
    Once the AI engine has analyzed all relevant tasks, the model’s status will update to Ready for Activation. A Benchmark value will be displayed, indicating the model’s reliability and effectiveness. This benchmark is calculated based on several factors, and the data science team can provide more details on its derivation.
  6. Model Management:
    • Activation: Once ready, the model can be activated and put into use. If needed, models can also be deactivated to accommodate shifting business needs.
    • Retraining the Model: If the benchmark is unsatisfactory, administrators can retain the model by adjusting the selected attributes. This flexibility ensures that the model remains relevant as business requirements evolve.
  7. Conclusion:

    The Intelligent Recommendations feature within Task Center introduces an AI-driven approach to task approvals, providing businesses with the ability to significantly reduce bottlenecks, enhance operational efficiency, and maintain compliance with critical deadlines. By allowing both business users and administrators to define confidence levels, manage models, and retrain them as necessary, this functionality empowers organizations to stay agile and competitive in an increasingly fast-paced market.

    As more providers are integrated into future releases, the scope of Intelligent Recommendations will continue to grow, offering even more opportunities for businesses to harness the power of AI in their workflows.

     

    Product Links:

    https://help.sap.com/docs/task-center/sap-task-center/working-with-intelligent-recommendations

    https://statics.teams.cdn.office.net/evergreen-assets/safelinks/1/atp-safelinks.html

    Enjoy!!

entry.title: Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build
entry.content:
Table of Content

  • Motivation for Guided Experiences

  • New Guided Process Modelling: Advanced Automation and Flexibility

  • Connecting Guided Processes as Wizard Templates in SAP Build Work Zone

  • Conclusion – SAP Build Guided Experiences

  • What's Next?

  • Additional Read

Motivation for Guided Experiences

Guided experiences are designed to simplify multi-step business processes by breaking them down into manageable and intuitive steps. Each step can be enriched with various types of content, including videos, documents, forms, tasks, UI cards, and apps to provide users with all the necessary information to complete each step. Steps can be classified as optional or mandatory, ensuring critical tasks are completed while allowing flexibility for other actions. Users don't need to worry about finishing all the steps at once as the progress is saved, enabling them to resume from where they left off. Guided experiences can be used for various complex processes such as parental leave, benefits enrolment, employee relocation, new hires onboarding or suppliers onboarding, training and enablement, and browsing and purchasing electronic equipment. To learn more about the background & motivation around guided experiences, please see our previous blog about this capability.

New Guided Process Modelling: Advanced Automation and Flexibility

The major enhancement in the October 2024 release is the ability to model guided processes directly in SAP Build Process Automation, bringing powerful process automation to your SAP Build projects. The following key aspects have been added:

Guided Process Artefact

The new ‘Guided Process’ artefact allows you to design and manage structured, multi-step processes within SAP Build Process Automation. This feature is perfect for creating more dynamic and reusable workflows.

Stages and Steps 

Following the structure introduced for the wizard layout, ‘Guided Processes’ equally allow to create stages & steps within each stage.  Additionally, these process steps can be designed to be sequential or parallel flows, allowing for both straightforward and complex workflows.

Create a StageAdd a Step to a Stage

 

Note: with this release, only SAP Build Work Zone is supported as ‘Step UI’; meaning the step itself needs to designed via the Work Zone wizard layout editor (for example to add UI integration cards).

Advanced Logic Integration

To create more sophisticated processes as basis for your guided experience, branches can be used to structure the overall guided process and duration checks can be incorporated via wait for duration to handle conditional starting of stages. Furthermore, subprocesses can be used to handle more complex requirements, such as retrieving hire date information.

Easy Deployment to SAP Build Work Zone

Once the guided process is modelled, it can be deployed to SAP Build Work Zone as a wizard layout template, ensuring easy availability for end-users. This provides a seamless transition from process design to execution.

Note: the deployment to SAP Build Work Zone is performed on behalf of the logged-in user via the automatically created destination pointing to DWS as per the following guide.

Shared Environments in SAP Build

This new ‘Guided Process’ artefact is only supported in shared environments, not in the default / public one. To learn more about shared environments in SAP Build, please refer to the following blog post.

Connecting Guided Processes as Wizard Templates in SAP Build Work Zone

In addition to modelling ‘wizard layouts’ directly in SAP Build Work Zone (which is now available via the “New Wizard” option)...

Add Wizard LayoutSelect Process for WizardOption for Standalone Wizard

 

… it is now possible to select ‘Guided Processes’ modelled in SAP Build Process Automation as the underlying structure when creating guided experiences.

Guided Process-based Wizards

Users can now select one of previously deployed guided processes they have access to. These serve as the template the wizard layout within SAP Build Work Zone.

Note: While modelling & deploying guided processes via the SAP Build Lobby requires access to the ‘advanced user’ license in SAP Build Process Automation, that is not the case for consuming deployed processes. For doing so, the user requires the following permissions:

  • Workpage edit permissions in Work Zone to edit the workpage, add wizard layouts,
  • ProcessAutomationParticipant role collection
  • 'Execute' permission for shared environment (Share an Environment | SAP Help Portal)

Note: creating wizard layouts based on ‘guided processes’ is currently released in preview environments of SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition / SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone and will become available in the non-preview environments with the subsequent release.

Auto-generated Wizard Structure

When selecting a guided process, the wizard layout is automatically generated based on the process definition, saving time and ensuring consistency.

Design Wizard with Widgets & UI Cards

Just like with a manually-created one, you can customize guided process-based wizard layouts using different widgets and UI cards, providing flexibility in designing the user experience.

  • Adding Widgets / Cards to each step: 
  • Example Step with Image widget:

Note: Already in a previous release, UI cards have been enhanced to interact with wizard layouts in SAP Build Work Zone, for instance to enforce users taking certain actions. Details and code examples for such UI cards are available via our central SAP Build Work Zone GitHub sample repository.

Process Instance Creation

When a user starts a wizard based on a guided process, an instance of that process is started automatically, allowing execution & resuming of the steps according to the defined process structure.

Conclusion – SAP Build Guided Experiences

This streamlined integration reduces development time and ensures that teams can leverage the power of SAP Build Process Automation alongside SAP Build Work Zone bringing exciting possibilities for creating powerful & content-rich guided experiences. These innovations not only simplify process design and execution but also ensure that you can deploy efficient, scalable guided experiences tailored to your unique needs – bringing different capabilities from SAP Build together via a deeply integrated, end-to-end experience!

What’s Next?

Looking ahead, we are planning to introduce additional features to further enhance guided experiences. These upcoming capabilities aim to add additional flexibility for creating, designing & operating guided experiences with SAP Build.  Here's a sneak peek at what's planned (*):

Spoiler
  • Repeating Guided Experiences
  • Starting Multiple Instances of the same Guided Experience
  • Support for more Step UIs, including Forms
  • Integration with Process Visibility for Monitoring
  • […]

In addition to those additional enhancements, we are also working on providing additional pre-built business content (packages) for guided experiences (*), such as onboarding in context of SAP SuccessFactors. Stay tuned as these new features roll out, offering even more ways to optimize your guided experiences and streamline your business processes!

(*) Disclaimer: Please note that the roadmap items shared here are subject to change and may not be delivered as initially planned. The information provided is for informational purposes only and does not constitute a commitment by SAP. As always, the best resource to learn about upcoming capabilities is the central roadmap explorer.

Additional Read

To learn more about the topic covered in this blog post, please also refer to the following assets & follow us on SAP community.

  • Related Blog Posts:
    • What’s new with SAP Build Work Zone guided experiences
    • SAP Build Process Automation - Introducing Environments
  • Learning Journeys & Certification: 
    • Work Zone: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning
    • Process Automation: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation
  • Reference Architectures:
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-sap-build-work-zone
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-build-process-automation
  • SAP Help guides for more details:
    • Work Zone Preview: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-advanced-edition/sap-build-work-zone-advanced-edition/what... (will move to regular admin guide with next release)
    • Process Automation: Create a Guided Process
  • GitHub Repository for Work Zone Examples: https://github.com/SAP-samples/build-workzone-integration/tree/main/advanced/wizard

entry.title: Your guide to Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build - Frequently asked questions
entry.content:
Welcome everyone! 

In this blogpost, I will breakdown the most frequently asked questions about guided experiences, an innovative capability powered by SAP Build. I will address questions around how it works, the benefits it offers, common use cases and much more. Let’s get started on unlocking the full potential of guided experiences. 

🔐For those looking to dive deeper into the capability, I recommend exploring our expert blogpost, where you'll find a comprehensive breakdown of its features. 

***

✔️ What are guided experiences in SAP Build?

Guided experience is a capability within SAP Build, a business application development and automation solution with a comprehensive suite of low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. This capability enables business users to simplify complex business processes into self-service journeys that are easy to follow and execute. It allows users to create interactive, step-by-step guides for navigating and using applications.

✔️ What are the key aspects of guided experiences?

Key aspects of guided experiences are as below. 

  • Step-by-step guidance: Users are guided through a series of steps to complete a specific task or process performed within SAP Build Work Zone with use cases often spanning across multiple systems. 
  • Customizable content: Users can create custom content for the guided experiences, including text, images, and videos, to provide clear and concise instructions, with no- and low-code capabilities.
  • Interactive elements: Guided experiences can include elements like UI Cards and default widgets (image, text, content, etc.) to engage users and ensure smooth execution.

✔️ What are some common use cases for guided experiences?

Some use cases examples can be: 

  • Creating one simple employee onboarding journey for new employees
  • Creating step-based guide to deliver best practices, training and relevant material to empower sales teams
  • Simplified purchase order verification process while purchasing equipment
  • Creating a smooth onboarding journey for new suppliers
  • Seamless journey for processing invoices across channel partners
  • And many more...

✔️Which SAP Build solutions are included in guided experiences?

Guided experiences incorporate capabilities from all the low-code SAP Build solutions - SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps.

✔️ How does guided experiences drive business value?

  • Enhanced User Experience: Empowers workforces with personalized digital experiences, boosting satisfaction, engagement, and retention.
  • Increased Productivity: Automates tasks and streamlines processes, enabling employees to focus on high-impact work.
  • Greater Agility: Rapidly deploys, modifies, and scale digital capabilities with intuitive tools, empowering business builders without IT dependency

✔️What is a 'wizard layout' in guided experiences?

Wizard layout the actual user interface in SAP Build Work Zone that the end-user work with. It allows users to create a step-by-step experience on any workpage. This layout can consist of one or multiple stages, each containing various steps. These steps or stages can be set as optional or mandatory, depending on the process requirements. Users can incorporate different UI elements available in Work Zone, such as tiles, widgets, UI cards, and other applications into each step.

✔️ What is a 'guided process' in guided experiences?

For more advanced processes, it is possible to create a ‘guided process’ as an artefact in SAP Build Process Automation and make use of the powerful capabilities of Process Automation. The guided process can then be deployed to SAP Build Work Zone and then used as the foundation/template when creating a wizard layout as an alternative option to creating the wizard manually directly in Work Zone.

✔️ What authorization is required for a user to create a Guided Experience in SAP Build Work Zone using a predefined template?

The user must have end-user permission/authorization. Any user with the permission to create and edit a work page can modify and create wizards within SAP Build Work Zone. When creating a guided experience wizard layout based on a ‘guided process’ template, the user requires access to a deployed template in either the public or another environment in Process Automation. To model and deploy the ‘guided process’ artefact, the advanced user permission is required in Process Automation side.

✔️ Can a user save their progress in SAP Build Work Zone for gradual competition?

Yes, progress of the user within the wizard layout is saved automatically so that they return and complete the steps another time – if needed.

✔️ Does the user mark completion of their own tasks or does Work Zone pick up on the status from other systems?

Work Zone has the ability to verify if a specific activity has been completed by the user. For mandatory steps, Work Zone ensures that the task is completed before allowing progress in the process. In each step, tasks / activities from other business systems could be incorporated via UI5 cards (where the task would then be completed). The UI card framework has also been enhanced to support interaction with the wizard layout to check the completion of a task. See here!

✔️ Are guided experiences available in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition?

No, they are not available in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and there are currently no plans to enable this capability in standard edition either.

✔️ Are guided experiences available in SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone?

 Yes, additionally to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, they are also available in SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. 

✔️ Given the visibility scenarios in SAP Build Process Automation, what additional benefits does SAP Build Work Zone offer?

SAP Build Work Zone provides the consumption layer and easy wizard editor that can be conveniently redefined by content administrator at runtime. With SAP Build Process Automation ‘guided process’ as the process backbone for the wizard layout, the flexible design & content capabilities of Work Zone provide the user-facing capabilities. Additionally, aspects like UI5 cards enable flexible, context-rich integration of business data across SAP and 3rd party solutions in each individual wizard step.

✔️ Is it possible to for the end-user to restart the completed process/step?

Capabilities to restart a completed process are on the roadmap, which can then be enabled in the guided experience configuration. Currently a guided experience can enforce a restart when editing and publishing a new version; alternatively, it can be configured to allow existing processes to complete with the previous version until completion.

✔️ What guided experience capabilities are on the roadmap?

Looking ahead into the H2 2024 and beyond, many additional capabilities are planned for the SAP Build guided experience capability. For instance integration with process visibility, support for additional step UIs like forms, ability for users to start multiple guided experiences for the same template and more.

Further to this, additional business content examples – for example onboarding from SAP SuccessFactors – are being created to get started more easily. This business content can then be used as template and tweaked as required.

***

I hope this blogpost has addressed your questions. If you want to learn more, feel free to explore the additional resources listed below.

➤ SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com / SAP Community

➤ SAP Build Process Automation on: sap.com / SAP Community 

➤ SAP Builders Community Group 

➤ Learning Journey: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning

➤ Learning Journey: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation

➤ SAP TechEd session ft. guided experiences: (AD108) Enhance Business Efficiency with SAP Build 

➤ SAP Devtoberfest session: Guided Experiences with SAP Build

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - Environments October Update
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Few months ago we introduced the Environments in this article. We've just released a significant update with the support of the following artifacts:

  • Process
  • Decision
  • Forms
  • Visibility Scenario

What it means for you?

You can now deploy a project containing a process within a Shared Environment. Only users who have access to a specific environment can use it. You can leverage the "Execute" privilege for enhancing the security and visibility of deployed Forms and Processes.

Two tabs have been added within the Control Tower > Environments for accessing Visibility Scenarios and Processes:

As explained before, now when deploying a new version, only the latest version will be seen as "active" within a Shared Environment. The behavior remains the same within the Public Environment.

For more details, our SAP Note has been updated with some useful information: https://me.sap.com/notes/3394899

entry.title: The Extensibility Wizard: One Tool to Rule Them All?
entry.content:
When creating clean core extensions for SAP S/4HANA, you are confronted with a vast number of powerful tools. The tools can be divided into four quarters by the dimensions on-stack versus side-by-side and low code versus pro-code, which is shown in the following figure:

The set of available tools may be overwhelming at first sight, so how to find the right one? And – if you found the right tool, how do you find the right API or extension point to extend the application you are interested in?

Of course, there are documents that provide guidance on when to use what (see for example in the document Extend SAP S/4HANA in the cloud and on premise with ABAP based extensions , Chapter 3, “When To Use What”), but it is definitely better to get the guidance right in the system when creation the extension. SAP is investing a lot in implementing guidance in the system. The investment goes along two paths:

  • Investment in AI support (Joule)
  • Investment in wizards

Many new wizards are available in the ABAP platform, for example for creating a new RAP BO or for creating custom fields. For a full picture, I refer to other documents and presentations given during TechEd and Devtoberfest 2024.

In this blog post I want to introduce the Extensibility Wizard. This wizard guides you from the application through the creation of the respective extension. Here is an example (instead of reading the following lines, you can also watch my demo in my TechEd or Devtoberfest session What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility 😞

  1. Assume that you are in your SAP S/4HANA application. For my example I choose the application Manage Journal Entries.
  2. Open the Fiori Launchpad context menu (click your avatar). If the Extensibility Wizard is configures (see link to the documentation with the setup guide at the end of this post), you can see the menu entry Create Extension, and you can click it (Figure 2).

                  
                  Figure 2

  1. The Extensibility Wizard opens and shows the different extensions tasks, that you can create for this application. As of 2408, the following extensions tasks are included:
    1. Custom Fields
    2. Custom Logic
    3. Custom Process
    4. The figure below shows – as a labs preview – also the extensibility task Custom Application, which is not yet available in your 2408 release.

               
               Figure 3

  1. Now let us select the extensibility task Custom Process. The next steps of the wizard now guide us through the creation of a new process in SAP Build Process Integration. As the wizard was started from the Manage Journal Entries application, it knows which triggers are relevant for this application, and offers for example the event JournalEntry Created as the trigger for a new process. As a final step, the wizard creates the new process in SAP Build and let you navigate to SAP Build Lobby and directly into the process. (Please see the complete flow in my session What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility )

I hope that this example gives you an idea on how the Extensibility Wizard works.

As mentioned, in 2408, the Extensibility Wizard includes – apart from the Custom Process – two more tasks:

  • The Custom Field task opens the Custom Fields Fiori app in your SAP S/4HANA tenant, where you can create a custom field. Also here, the business context is prefilled according to the application from where you start the wizard.
  • The Custom Logic task opens the Custom Logic Fiori app in your SAP S/4HANA tenant, where you can create custom logic. Also here, the business context is prefilled.

If you are used to open the Custom Fields and the Custom Logic apps via the UI Adaption Mode (menu entry Adapt UI, as you can see in Figure 2), you can of course still do this.

In future additional extensibility items and features will be added, for example:

  • For the extensibility item Custom Process, additional trigger options will be provided.
  • A new extensibility item Custom Application will be added, which guides you through the creation of a new application with SAP Build Code.

Curious to see the wizard in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud tenant? Configure it today, see the documentation here: https://help.sap.com/docs/build/sap-build-core/use-extensions?version=Cloud

entry.title: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition bundle
entry.content:
GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition

Since March 2023, customers who have selected SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as the core ERP system to run their businesses have received additional entitlements beyond the core functionality. These added capabilities form one of two comprehensive bundles: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base and GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium.
 
In this blog, I will focus on the base edition bundle, providing a clear explanation of each entitlement and its business value. I will discuss the premium edition in a separate blog, which I will hyperlink here once created.

This blog is designed for business users and consultants with ERP experience, aiming to provide practical insights into each entitlement's purpose and when it’s most valuable. Please note, statements about adoption difficulty and required personas are based on my research and experience, and other cases may vary based on the specific business context etc.
Note 1: Hyperlinks to SAP4ME will take you directly to the relevant page if you're logged into a productive SAP4ME system and have suitable rights
Note 2: This blog speaks to the entitlements included as of 2024-10-17

Blog Outline:

  1. GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Base: Overview
  2. Core Entitlement: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  3. General Notes on Additional Entitlements
  4. Base Entitlement: Finance - Group Reporting
  5. Base Entitlement: BTP - SAP Build Process Automation
  6. Base Entitlement: BTP - SAP Build Workzone, Standard Edition
  7. Base Entitlement: BTP - CPEA Credits
  8. Base Entitlement: SAP Digital Access
  9. Base Entitlement: Ariba Buying, Base Edition
  10. Closing Remarks

 

  1. GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Base: Overview
    The Base Edition comes in two SKUs: 8014771 for global markets and 8014782 for the Greater China region (which excludes some entitlements). This blog focuses on SKU 8014771.

    How to identify if you have this SKU:
    1. Log in to SAP for Me: Reporting > Purchased Solutions
    2. Under 'Orders by Solution Area,' select S/4HANA Public - your SKU will be listed on the right. If 8014771 appears with a valid date, you have the GROW Base Edition. If 8014761 appears, you have Premium. Other SKUs likely represent SKUs that do not include this full suite of entitlements.

  2. Core Entitlement: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    The core entitlement includes:
    1. Services & Support: Access to support services like incident creation, onboarding advisor support, and the SAP Enterprise Support Portfolio, which offers learning content and services such as Continuous Quality Checks.
    2. Systems: Access to multiple systems (e.g., ERP production, test, and development systems) and related tenants. This also includes non-ERP systems like Cloud ALM, CBC, SAP for Me, and BTP systems.
    3. Functionality: Access to pre-configured scope items. Commonly used ones include J59 - Accounts Receivable and J60 - Accounts Payable. You can view and manage scope items through SAP Activate’s Availability and Dependencies of Scope Items accelerator.
    4. Full User Equivalent (FUE): Your contract includes a specific number of FUEs that govern how many business users can access core functionality. Check your licensed FUE quantity in SAP for Me: Reporting - License Consumption under metric CY334.
    5. Entitlement Measurement: Overuse is possible but monitored by SAP. Your account team will reach out in case of overconsumption.

  3. General Notes on Additional Entitlements
    What is an "entitlement"?
    An entitlement refers to the right to use a specific set of functionalities that normally requires additional licensing (at a cost). In the GROW Base Edition, certain entitlements are included up to a threshold without extra software licensing costs. For example, get an entitlement to use up to 25 objects with the Group Reporting Functionality - this blog goes through entitlements like this which are included in your bundle.

    Are the "entitlements" completely free?
    The outlined entitlements mean there's no software licensing cost if you use up to the amount indicated. Usage beyond that quantity will require additional (paid) licenses, and you may need to consider some investment of business time and effort to adopt new features and functions, and in some cases you'll require consultants for the setup and implementation.

    What does "adoption" mean in this blog?
    I'm using 'adoption' to refer to the setup AND usage a software functionality. The setup or implementation is the process of adopting, and once it's setup and being actively used  - then I would then say the customer has adopted a feature/entitlement.
    Base Edition - Additional Entitlements:
    GROW with SAP Public Edition, base - additional entitlements
  4. Base Edition Entitlement: Group Reporting
    Business Case
    Group Reporting helps businesses that operate multiple subsidiaries or legal entities shorten the time needed for financial consolidation. It automates key activities and streamlines the data model, making it highly valuable for businesses with complex financial structures. For single-entity businesses, this functionality is not immediately relevant.

    Business Role Relevance
    Senior finance roles, such as Finance Managers and Group Controllers, will benefit from this functionality for managing financial consolidations.

    Entitlement
    The GROW Base Edition includes 25 Group Reporting objects. An object typically refers to a legal entity within a consolidation group (e.g., parent and subsidiary companies). Additional licenses are required for more than 25 objects. Official documentation is here

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption using metric CH112 in SAP for Me under SAP4ME: Reporting -> License Consumption.

    Overusage
    It is possible to over-consume your allocated Group Reporting objects. SAP will periodically review your usage under metric CH112. You can license additional units by contacting SAP for SKU 8013710.

    Scope Item Relevance
    The main scope item for Group Reporting is 1SG. This measures whether data from group reporting has been posted into the system. You can validate its usage in Portfolio & Products - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Usage. Other Scope Items flagged as Group Reporting relevant are  287, 28B, 4R7, 2U6

    More Information
    For more detailed guidance on adopting Group Reporting, refer to this blog post. This deeper descriptions, webinar links, and crucially it outlines the key steps to adopting group reporting

    Key Adoption Notes:
    Expertise needed: You'll need a combination of BTP administrators and finance consultants for configuration and initial setup.
    Adoption Effort:  Rated 3 (on a scale from 1 to 5). You’ll need finance consultants, and technical consultants with expertise in BTP to set up this functionality.
    Not to be confused with: This entitlement does not include Group Reporting Data Collection, which must be licensed separately.

  5. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Build Process Automation
    Business Case
    SAP Build Process Automation enables non-developers, such as key users or business process experts, to create bots or automated workflows based on a set of rules. This is valuable when no standard ERP functionality exists to meet a specific business need, for example, automating the creation of general ledger accounts.

    Business Role Relevance
    Consultants, process automation specialists, and business key users involved in implementing and running the ERP system will find this useful.

    Entitlement
    The base edition includes:
    20 Standard Users (CP278) – Can trigger or interact with a process.
    2 Advanced Users (CP279) – Can design, monitor, and administrate processes.
    1 Unattended Automation (CP283) – A bot that continuously monitors and executes a process.
    Documentation of these entitlements are here

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption of CP278, CP279, and CP283 in SAP4ME - Reporting - License

    Overusage
    You are blocked from overconsuming these licenses. Once the entitlement limit is reached, additional licenses must be purchased or allocated through CPEA credits.

    Scope Item Relevance
    There is no specific scope item for SAP Build Process Automation.

    More Information
    This community page has overview videos and links to all other resources. This area of the API hub is where you find SAP pre-created templates.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: You’ll need a BTP expert for the base setup. Then you'll need LoB experts e.g. finance experts to develop meaningful automations as per your needs. Setup steps are outlined here.
    Adoption Effort: 2/5 for platform setup by BTP experts; 2-4/5 for workflow creation depending on complexity.

  6. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Build Workzone, Standard Edition

    Business Case
    SAP Build Workzone allows organizations to create a central homepage for employees with user-specific access to applications across various SAP systems. This enhances efficiency by providing direct links to necessary business apps for roles like sales managers or purchasing clerks. The more SAP systems used throughout your organization, the more value this solution will offer your organization.

    Business Role Relevance
    This functionality benefits employees across departments who frequently use SAP applications in their daily tasks.

    Entitlement
    You receive 1 Workzone user per FUE licensed. For example, if you have licensed 100 FUEs for the core, 100 users can access SAP Build Workzone.

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption of CP264 in SAP4ME - Reporting - License Consumption

    Overusage
    Additional licenses or CPEA credits are required if the number of users exceeds your entitlement.

    Scope Item Relevance
    There is no scope item related to SAP Build Workzone usage.

    More Information
    More documentation is on the SAP Build Workzone Discovery Center.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: A BTP global administrator for initial setup, followed by non-technical key users for managing pages.
    Adoption Effort: 2/5 for BTP experts; 3/5 for non-experts. View the setup steps in this tutorial.
    Not to be confused with: SAP Build Workzone is distinct from SAP Start, which a more lightweight solution without ability to consider other SAP system authorizations. SAP Build Workzone Advanced Edition is a different product without a migration path from the standard edition.

  7. Base Edition Entitlement: CPEA Credits

    Business Case
    CPEA Credits (Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement) allow businesses to consume various BTP services within predefined limits without additional fees. These credits can be applied to services like Integration Suite, SAP Build Apps, and Cloud Identity Services. You can view the list of included services here in the Discovery Center. There's more nuance to the CPEA credit commercial model, which you can read about here.

    Business Role Relevance
    Technical teams managing BTP services will oversee consumption of this entitlement. The business areas that consume this depend on which services you're utilizing from BTP - many are in the technical / administration area.

    Entitlement
    Customers receive 1% of their net Annual Contract Value in CPEA credits, with a minimum value of €2,000 and a maximum value of €16,000.

    License Consumption
    You can view your CPEA credit balance in SAP4ME - Finance and Legal - Enterprise Agreements

    Scope Item Relevance
    CPEA credit usage is not tied to any scope item, as it applies across various BTP services.

    More Information
    Each service that can be paid for by CPEA credits are listed here in the Discovery Center - and this same resource provides additional information on each service.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: BTP experts and business area owners are required to manage and utilize CPEA credits effectively.
    Adoption Effort: Varies depending on the BTP service being used.
    Not to be confused with: CPEA credits differ from BTPEA and EA credits, which operate similarly but cover different services.

  8. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Digital Access

    Business Case
    SAP Digital Access governs how non-human interactions (such as external systems creating documents) are licensed. It is relevant when integrating third-party systems like Salesforce to trigger document creation within SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

    Business Role Relevance
    IT and integration specialists responsible for system integrations with non-SAP platforms will need to manage this entitlement.

    Entitlement
    The base edition includes 100,000 digital access documents per year.

    License Consumption
    Track your consumption by launching the License Compliance Digital Access app in the productive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. This guidance document shows how to do this and provides other useful context on measuring your digital access usage for SAP S/4HANA

    Overusage
    It is possible to exceed your annual document allowance. SAP periodically reviews your consumption and the account team will reach out in the case of overconsumption.

    Scope Item Relevance
    This entitlement applies to external system interactions and is not tied to specific scope items.

    More Information
    Refer to the Digital Access documentation for a more detailed explanation.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: No specific competency is needed. Entitlement consumption should be reviewed by the party responsible for your SAP system billing & commercials.
    Adoption Effort: 1. There's nothing to setup per se for Digital Access - you only be aware of it and monitor your consumption.
    Other Notes: SAP Digital Access is only necessary for non-SAP systems. If SAP systems trigger document creation, digital access is not consumed.

  9. Base Edition Entitlement: Ariba Buying, Base Edition

    Business Case
    Ariba Buying Base Edition allows employees to make internal purchase requisitions. It's scope is similar to the core ERP requisitioning scope of 18J, but Ariba Buying Base has an enhanced UX and has the capability to integrate with many advanced features, such as directly connecting to supplier catalogs and the Ariba Business network.

    Business Role Relevance
    All company employees can benefit from an improved user experience for purchase requisitions, particularly in organizations using self-service requisitioning.

    Entitlement
    There is no access limit for the base functionality, but additional licensing may be required for extended capabilities, such as linking to external supplier catalogs.

    License Consumption
    There are no limits to usage of this entitlement. It operates more like a standard scope item, but many of the functionalities that bring Ariba Buying Base to life will require additional licenses.

    Scope Item Relevance
    Scope item 4AI measures whether you've setup the communications arrangement required to use Ariba Buying base edition.

    More Information
    View an overview of Ariba Buying in this video demo - but note this video includes the utilization of catalogs that may require additional licensing.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: BTP administrators and procurement experts are required for setup and catalog integration.
    Adoption Effort: Unclear
    Other Notes: A lot of the functionality’s value comes from adding catalogs and integrating with the broader Ariba Network, which may require additional licensing.

  10. Closing
    The intent of this blog was to give you a base understanding of the each entitlement in the base edition - what it is, and when it's most valuable.
    Any feedback regarding on the content including other use cases, other resources to link etc. are welcome and I'll periodically update this blog with improved information.

entry.title: Configuring a destination for Action Editor
entry.content:
Motivation:

This blog focuses on the concept of destinations available for testing an Action within Action Project.

 

Parent Blog:

Actions Editor – Feature List, Usage and Maintenance of Actions Project | SAP Blogs

 

Concept & Need:

Actions can be executed by using destinations created in the subscriber's subaccount. Multiple destinations can be created in the cockpit, but not all of them are relevant for an Action Project, so filtering is necessary.

 

Destination Configuration:

In order to make a destination relevant for an Action Project, you should add a additional property "sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled" with a value “true” in the destination configuration. For projects created using a live API source, add another property "sap.build.usage" with the corresponding value from the table below to the destination configuration.

API SourceValue
Graph"GRAPH"
CAP"CAP"
RAP"RAP"
BTP"odata_gen"
Abhe"apihub_enterprise"
IFlow"integration_flow_rt"

Figure 1: Destination configuration

Note: For the projects created using API specification as source, all the destination with "sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled" property as true will be available.

 

Making a Destination available:

To enable a destination for testing an Action in Action Editor, the following process must be followed in control tower.

Navigate to control tower from lobby

Figure 2: Control tower button

Navigate to destinations inside control tower

Figure 3: Destinations button

Using the add functionality add the desired destination

Figure 4: Add destination button

The added destination will be available for selection in the destination dropdown present in test tab

Figure 5: Destination selection dropdown

 

Blog Reference:

    • Part 1 of the Blog Series: The New Actions Project – Major Changes including the Actions Editor Available Now !! | SAP Blogs
    • Part 2 of the Blog Series/Parent Blog: Actions Editor – Feature List, Usage and Maintenance of Actions Project

 

Thanks for reading and I hope it helped to understand the concept of object flattening within the Actions Editor. Please feel free to leave a comment if there are any questions and I would be happy to receive any feedback.

entry.title: You ask, we answer - Ask Me Anything Session (Oct 24)
entry.content:
During SAP TechEd 2024, SAP unveiled significant improvements to SAP Build, which included making SAP S/4HANA extension development more efficient, introducing new generative AI features, and providing broader access to ABAP development tools. These changes empower the developer community to innovate more easily and with increased agility. 

You can read all about the announcements in Michael Ameling's blog post: Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud! And the good news is: you can still access the session catalog to review the content and stay current with the latest innovations & enjoy Devtoberst sessions until October 25, check out the agenda here.

Now if you're curious about what you saw during the event and have some more questions, or you would like to dive deeper, here's a great opportunity:

We'll be hosting a live Ask Me Anything session on October 24, 3pm CET. You can watch it on YouTube, or click the preview above. In the session, the panel of experts will recap the features announced and answer your questions live. 

Can't join? No worries, drop your questions in the comments and the experts will answer them live for you to watch later 😉

Looking forward to seeing you there!

Cheers,
Maiara

entry.title: SAP Build Process AutomationでJava Scriptが使用できるようになりました。
entry.content:
SAP Build Process Automationの2024/10のアップデートにより新たに「スクリプトタスク」が使用できるようになりました。これまでは自動化（RPA）の中でJava Scriptを使用する事が出来ましたが、今回追加されたのはプロセス（＝ワークフロー）上でJava Scriptを実行できる機能です。なかなか便利な機能ですのでこれの紹介と使用に関するポイントを解説したいと思います。

スクリプトタスクはプロセス上で使用可能なタスクです。

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

この様にフォームなどと同様に追加することが出来ます。

 

スクリプトの編集用のエディタです。サンプルというか例のようなコードがコメントアウトされた形で入っています。（これは削除したほうが良いでしょうね。）

ここに実行したいJavaScriptのコードを書いていく形になります。
どのようなコードを書くのか？ですが、基本的には「カスタム変数に値を代入するコード」となります。
現在、書ける（実行できる）コードには制限があります。重要なものを挙げますと

  • フォームなどで設定しているフィールドの値は「読み取り専用の変数」としてアクセス可能です。上書きは出来ません。
  • カスタム変数に対しては読み取り・代入（上書き）が可能です。
  • 言語仕様としてはECMA 5.1のJava Scriptに従い、サーバーサイドJava Scriptとして実行されます。
  • importやRequire等で外部のライブラリを読み込むということは出来ません。

フォーム等のフィールドの値はコンテキストログとしても保管されるユーザーさんが入力した情報です。これは監査等のためにログを使用する場合を考えると、上書き出来ないというのは納得できるかなと思います。
私はこのスクリプトの中で外部のサービスにアクセスしてそこから情報を取ってということが出来ないかなと試してみたのですが、これは厳しそうです。ImportやRequireが使えないのと、FetchではPromiseの問題でエラーとなります。外部へのアクセスを行いたいなら素直にActionを使用するべきでしょう。

スクリプトの中でフォームやカスタム変数にアクセスするには

$.context.<Formname>.<Fieldname>
のような形でアクセスします。注意事項としてトリガー（フォーム）の値は
$.context.startEvent.<Fieldname>
 です。しかしながら直接タイピングするのではなくスクリプトエディタ右側の

 

 

 

 

 

 

プロセス一覧で表示されているフィールド名、変数名をクリックするとコードにその名称が代入されますのでこれを利用するべきでしょう。

カスタム変数にスクリプトから代入できるということで、以前私が書いたデータ型の変換 等はより簡単になります。ステップが減らせそうですね。

スクリプトのテスト実行もこのボタンから行うことも出来ます。
しかし、このコードの場合$.context.custom.customvariable1や$.context.form_form1_1.num1は本来ワークフロープロセスインスタンスで取得する値なのでうまく実行することが出来ません。そのため

Test Variableタブの方でそれらの変数の宣言と初期値の代入を行う必要があります。
テスト実行してみましょう。

実行できました。カスタム変数には数値型フィールドの値を文字列型に変換した値と指定した文字列が連結されて代入されています。

テスト実行の際には少々注意が必要です。もしコードエディタ中でコードが選択状態になっている場合、その部分だけがテスト実行されます。これは便利といえば便利ですが何故うまく実行されないんだ？と悩むポイントになるかもしれません。意図的にコードの一部分を実行させたいならともかく、そうでないならテスト実行前に何処か別の場所をクリックしてコードを選択状態から外し実行するという癖をつけたほうが良いかもしれません。また、私が試した感じだとこのデバッグ実行はブラウザのJavaScriptで実行される感があります。（toLocalString()等の挙動から）。本来はサーバーサイドJavaScriptで実行されるはずですので挙動の違いが有りました。書式のチェック程度に留めてテスト環境へのデプロイで再度テストは必要でしょう。

なお、スクリプトタスクのスクリプトではreturn等のコードは必要ありません。このスクリプトタスクで例えば何か計算を行ってその結果をプロセスの次のフェーズで使いたいという場合は、カスタム変数にその結果を代入して使用するという形を取る必要があります。そのため、大半の場合コードの終わりはカスタム変数への値の代入という形になります。

コードエディタではインテリセンスのような自動補完機能が効きますので私は少々遊んでみました。
どうもSAP Workflow Managementのプロパティが使えるようです。
https://help.sap.com/docs/workflow-capability/workflow-cloud-foundry/cheat-sheet-for-workflow-expressions
その中で現在のSAP Build Process Automationでは簡単には取得できないなあという物がありましたので紹介します。プロセス中で"その前の承認や申請が完了した時刻"というのは実は参照が難しいという問題があります。これは行うとしたらアクションを用いてSAP Build Process AutomationのAPIにアクセスしてログから取得という形になると考えられますが、これが実は簡単に取得できます。

$.usertasks.<Formname>.last.completedAt

という変数を参照するとそのフォーム（承認含む）の完了日時を参照することが出来ます。
(<Formname>の部分はそのフォームの変数にアクセスする際の$.context.<Formname>.<Fieldname>のFormname>と同じですのでそれを使用して下さい。）

私がテストしたところなのですが、テスト実行ではtoLocaleString()でのロケール指定の書式・時刻変換が効くのですが、デプロイ後はtoLocaleString('ja-JP')のようにロケールを指定してもJSTや日本の日付書式に変換ができませんでした。そのため上記の様に手動で+9時間を行ったり、日付書式を日本で使用される形式に変換しています。

トリガーの日時は取得できませんので必要な場合はトリガー後にカスタム変数に現在の日時を設定するスクリプトタスクを記述して下さい。

過去、私はSAP Build Process Automationのちょっとしたテクニック　ー　変数定義 という記事を公開しました。その中でDecisionを利用した変数設定方法を解説しました。実は現在の日時（処理を行った日時）の取得と代入（設定）というのはDecisionを使用した方法では行うことが出来ません。スクリプトタスクを使用すると日時を変数で持つことが出来るのでそれを利用して処理や表示を変えるという事もできます。

パワーユーザーの方、ぜひスクリプトタスクを使いこなして下さい。

entry.title: Integrate SAP Build Process Automation with Custom Business Object from S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Overview

This post is an update on the blog to setup an extension scenario with on-stack and Side-by-Side Extensions for SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

The use case shown here is creating a Bonus Plan application in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud as a Custom Business Object. This application will initiate the SAP Build Process Automation workflow, allowing the manager to Approve/Reject newly produced Bonus Plans.

Bonus Plan application which is a Custom Business Object and its ABAP Business Logic is developed as an In-App Extension running on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, whereas the workflow is developed using SAP Build Process Automation which is the unified offering in SAP BTP that combines the capabilities of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA and its starting point can be FORM or API. You can read more on SAP Build Process Automation (here).

Note: The Bonus Plan app shown in the exercises is for educational purpose only and not meant for productive usage. It is not ensured that the data used in this extension scenario is managed in accordance with applicable legal requirements for all countries and industries and in accordance with the business needs, such as data life cycle requirements.

Prerequisite:
1.Subscription of SAP Build process automation in BTP Trial Account or BTP Enterprise Account
2.Your user needs a business role with business catalog " Extensibility – Custom Business Objects (ID: SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_CBO)” in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Let’s start on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud side with the definition of the Custom Business Object and the communication settings.

Custom Business Object

Define the data structure and create the database persistence of a custom business object.

  1. Enter your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system’s Fiori Launchpad.
  2. Start typing Custom Business Objects in the Launchpad search and open the App from the results.
  3. Press New to start the creation of a custom business object.
  4. Enter the Name BonusPlan. The other editable fields will be filled up automatically with suggested values but can be changed. From Features section Determination and Validation, User Interface and Back End Service to be selected. With the User Interface option, it generates an SAP Fiori based UI for your Custom Business Object and with the Back End Service option, it generates an OData API that you can expose and access outside with the help of communication arrangement. 

5. Switch to the Fields tab. On the BONUSPLAN node we need the following fields:

Here Approver field has association to Business Object “WORKFORCEPERSON_VH”.

6. The Release Status is linked to the following Custom Code List:

  • To create "YY1_RELSTAT00" custom code list launch "Custom Reusable Elements" app from the Fiori Launchpad.
  • Under Custom Code Lists, choose Add (+) and enter the following details:
  • Name: RELSTAT00, Code List ID: YY1_RELSTAT00
  • Under CODE VALUES, create the Items as mentioned in the picture.

With these settings you have defined the data structure of the Custom Business Object out of which the corresponding database tables and CDS Views will be created during Publish. Publish the business object to trigger the generation of UI (Master and Detail) and OData Service. 

  • Select the just added Catalog and Publish it.

  • open the ABAP editor for the Request Approval action by clicking the Request Approval link and here we can add logic to trigger workflow in SAP Build Process Automation

Initially the action is empty, so you add the following coding.

Note: 3 where we have mentioned how to get the workflow definitionId and context payload structure for triggering the Business process workflow in the BTP.

*Here we are using CL_SWF_CPWF_API_FACTORY_A4C API class to start workflows in SAP Build Process Automation. For more info, please refer to this link.

 

* Action RequestApproval for Node ID BONUSPLAN
** Importing Parameter : association (Navigation to Parent/Child/Associated Node Instances)
*  write (API for creating and updating Custom Business Object Node Instances)
* Changing Parameter  : BONUSPLAN (Current Node Data)
* Exporting Parameter : message (Message with Severity S(uccess), W(arning), E(rror))  

* Get the API of workflow.
  DATA(cpwf_api) = cl_swf_cpwf_api_factory_a4c=>get_api_instance( ).
  DATA Wf_handle     TYPE sysuuid_c32.

CONSTANTS: wf_definitionId   TYPE if_swf_cpwf_api=>cpwf_def_id_long  VALUE '****************************.custombonusplan.bonus_change_wf',   
             wf_retention_days TYPE if_swf_cpwf_api=>retention_time VALUE '30',
             consumer          TYPE string VALUE 'DEFAULT'.

**** Define the structure of the payload
  TYPES: BEGIN OF context, 
           approveremail     		TYPE string, 
           id                		TYPE string,
           Bonus             		TYPE string,  
           EmployeeID        		TYPE string, 
           EmployeeName  		TYPE string, 
           ReleaseStatus     		TYPE string, 
           releasestatustext 		TYPE string, 
           DecisionNote      		TYPE string,
           TargetRevenue    		TYPE string,   
           sapuuid           		TYPE string,
         END OF context.

  TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_context,
           context TYPE context,
         END OF ty_context.

 TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_payload,
           start_event TYPE ty_context,
 END OF ty_payload  .

* Get the Email Address for the Approver
  SELECT SINGLE from I_BUSINESSUSERVH FIELDS DEFAULTEMAILADDRESS
    WHERE BPIDENTIFICATIONNUMBER = @BONUSPLAN-approver
    INTO (approvermailid).

* Get the SAP UUID for the BONUSPLAN instance
SELECT SINGLE from YY1_BONUSPLAN FIELDS sap_uuid
    WHERE id = @BONUSPLAN-id
    INTO (uuid).

NEW cl_system_uuid( )->convert_uuid_x16_static(
       EXPORTING
          uuid     = uuid
       IMPORTING
          uuid_c36 = DATA(lv_c36 ) ).
  TRY.
* Get the API of workflow
      DATA(lo_cpwf_api) = cl_swf_cpwf_api_factory_a4c=>get_api_instance( ).
*
**convert workflow data to be passed in json format
**Make sure the approvermailid should be Assigned to Process Automation Roles in the BTP
      DATA(context)   = VALUE type_context(
            context-approveremail    = approvermailid	
             context-id              	= bonusplan-id
            context-bonus           	= |{ bonusplan-targetbonus_v }|
            context-employeeid      	= |{ bonusplan-employeeid }|
            context-employeename   = |{ bonusplan-employeename }|
            context-releasestatus  	 = |{ bonusplan-releasestatus }|
            context-releasestatustext = 'Approval Required'
            context-decisionnote    	 = ''
            context-targetrevenue      = |{ bonusplan-targetrevenue_v }|
            context-sapuuid               =  |guid'{ lv_c36 }'|
             ).

      DATA(context_json) = lo_cpwf_api->get_start_context_from_data(
        iv_capital_letter  = abap_false
        it_name_mapping    = VALUE #( ( abap = 'CONTEXT' json = 'bonusContext' ) )
        iv_data = context
      ).

*trigger workflow
      DATA(cpwf_handle_id) = lo_cpwf_api->start_workflow_ext(
              iv_cp_workflow_def_id = wf_definitionId
              iv_context            = context_json
              iv_retention_time     = wf_retention_days
              iv_do_commit          = abap_true
            ).
      wf_handle = cpwf_handle_id.  
    CATCH cx_swf_cpwf_api INTO DATA(lx_exc).
      message = VALUE #(
    severity = co_severity-success
    text     = 'Action ApproveBonus failed'
  ).

ENDTRY.
message = VALUE #(
    severity = co_severity-success
    text     = 'Action ApproveBonus executed'
).

 

 

Creation of a Communication Arrangement for SAP Build Process Automation Workflows

To integrate custom business object from SAP S/4HANA system with External Systems or Services like SAP Build Process Automation you need to create a Communication Arrangement.

A Communication Arrangement describes which communication partners communicate with each other in the scenario, and how they communicate. Communication Arrangement is formed by combining the below artifacts:

1. Custom Communication Scenario

The setup of the communication (inbound and outbound) between SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP starts with the definition of a Custom Communication Scenario.
The Custom Communication Scenario contains the information for both inbound and outbound HTTP-calls.

  • Open the app Custom Communication Scenarios in the Launchpad and create a new Custom Communication Scenario with the name YY1_BPWORKFLOW.

  • On the tab Inbound Services, you create an entry for your Custom Business Object: Press Add button to add inbound service “YY1_BONUSPLAN_CDS” and click select button. 

     

  • On the Outbound Services tab you create an entry for the workflow capability on SAP BTP with the URL-Path /workflow-service/rest/v1

    Switch to Outbound Service section Press Add to start outbound service creation. A pop-up opens. Enter following data into the input fields.

Field Label

Field Value

Description

Outbound Service for SAP Build Process Automation

Outbound Service ID

BONUSAPPROVAL (prefix YY1_ and suffix _REST are added automatically)

URL Path

/workflow-service/rest/v1(service specific part of before gotten service’s end point)

 

Press Create to finish the outbound service creation.

Press Save and Publish to finish the custom communication scenario creation.

2. Communication User:

If you want to access the BonusPlan OData service (Inbound) you also need to setup an authentication method. You can use several methods like e.g.­., Basic Authentication, Certificate or OAuth.

In this case we are choosing Basic Authentication for which you need to setup a Communication User. Open the app Maintain Communication Users from the Communication Management group and create there a new user with username, description, and password.

3. Create Communication Arrangement

You will now create a communication arrangement between the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and SAP Build process automation using service key of SAP Build process automation Instance.

For this, follow the following steps:

a) Get SAP Build Process Automation Service Key

  • In the SAP BTP cockpit, navigate to your space. See Navigate to Orgs and Spaces.
  • From the navigation pane, choose Instances and Subscriptions.
  • Click the name of your SAP Build Process Automation instance.
  • From the navigation pane, choose Service Keys.
  • Select one entry and copy the service key.

b) Create Communication Arrangement using Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0863

Communication Scenario: Communication Scenario is a design time description of how two communication partners communicate with each other. It consists of technical information like inbound and/or outbound services example OData or SOAP as well as supported authentication methods. There are various standard communication scenarios provided by SAP which are ready to use, like in this blog we would be using SAP_COM_0863 (SAP Process Automation Integration).

  • Open the Communication Arrangement app in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system’s Fiori Launchpad
  • Click on New to create communication arrangement.
  • Choose Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0863. SAP_COM_0863 is a ready-to-use communication scenario provided by SAP for integration with SAP Build Process Automation.
  • Select a Communication User (of which you know the password) which has created in the previous step or create a new one to use for inbound communication. 

  • Click on Additional Properties, set given value, and click Close.

       Consumer Type (Workflow) – DEFAULT

The consumer type is used to identify the default communication arrangement i.e., if the SAP Build Process Automation Communication Arrangement has Consumer Type (Workflow) set to DEFAULT, then the system will try to trigger all workflows in SAP Build Process Automation by default. Therefore, default assignment can be done only on one Communication arrangement for a Consumer Type.

  • In Service key text area paste the SAP Build Process Automation instance service key that we retrieved as part of ‘Get SAP Build Process Automation Service Key’ (See Section 3.a) and choose Create.

c) Create Communication Arrangement for Custom Communication Scenario

As the last part the Custom Communication Arrangement brings all these previously created information together. It contains the information for both inbound and outbound HTTP calls.

Open the app Communication Arrangements and create there a new one based on the previously created Custom Communication Scenario YY1_BPWORKFLOW.

Here you save the inbound service URL for the destination configuration in BTP.

Build your Business Process Workflow with SAP Build Process Automation

Enable users to create forms, manage decision logic, and build, adapt, and organize process flows with the simplicity of drag-and-drop functionality. You will learn how to use forms to build Bonus Approval workflow in this blog. The Approver must review these Bonus Approval requests before approving or rejecting them. The requester will be notified of the decision.

1. Create destination to SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.    

In the SAP BTP cockpit, you need to create destinations to enable the communication to the S/4HANA Cloud system. In addition, the destination must be enabled for use in SAP Build Process Automation.

Procedure

In the SAP BTP cockpit, navigate to your subaccount. See Navigate to Orgs and Spaces.

Choose Connectivity > Destination > Create Destination.

Choose Blank Template and enter the following data and click on Save.

Field

Value

Name

Enter the destination name.

Type

Select HTTP.

Description

Enter a description (SBPA to ABAP Callback Destination)

URL

Enter the API-URL saved during Communication Arrangement creation. If not saved access the communication arrangement

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

Select BasicAuthentication.

User

(Inbound) communication user used in the communication arrangement

Password

Set the password to the secret. Password of the (inbound) communication user used in the communication arrangement.

Additional Properties

Add the following properties:

“sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled” → true

“sap.build.usage” -> RAP

“sap.processautomation.enabled ” → true

  • Save your entries.

2. Add Callback destination in SAP Build process Automation.

  • Open the SAP Build Process automation from your subaccount.

Subaccount > Instances and Subscriptions > Subscriptions > SAP Build Process Automation

  • Click on SAP Build Process Automation to open the Application.
  • Navigate Control Tower > Destinations.
  • Select the destination that you have created in the SAP BTP cockpit and click Add.

3. Create a Business Process Workflow in SAP Build Process Automation.

  • Launch SAP Build Process Automation application from the BTP and In the Lobby, choose Create.
  • Pick Build an Automated Process.
  • Select Business Process.

Business Process Projects are a collection of skills in SAP Build Process Automation. Projects are part of the internal business processes of a company and are defined based on business scenarios. A project can contain a set of processes, forms, automations and decisions, which are reusable artifacts.

  • In the Create a Business Process Project dialog box, do the following:
  • Enter a Project Name: custombonusplan
  • Enter a Short Description: custom bonus plan
  • Choose Create
  • Create a business process
  • A new tab opens with the newly created project.
  • In the Create Process dialog box, provide the following:
  • Enter a Name: bonus_change_wf
  • Enter a Description for your process: Process to handle bonus Workflow
  • Choose Create

4.  Create the data type for the API Trigger.

  • Go to your project and click on Create > Data Type.
  • Name the data type Bonus Context and click on Create.
  • Click on New Field to add new fields to the data type Bonus Context. Your final data type looks as below. Click Save to save the data type.

 

5. Create an API Trigger for the process.

Create an API trigger for the business process to be called from external system

Process triggers can be a form, such as a request form, an API call, where an external system starts the process or an Event. You may also start the instance with an event trigger which on-boards and listens to back-end events emitted from an external source system and reacts to such events by triggering artifacts such as processes and automations.

  • Open process bonus_change_wf.
  • To add an API trigger for the process, click on Add API trigger.
  • Choose Call an API as we are creating an API based trigger so that we can trigger the process from external system like S4HANA or SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

  • Enter the below details for the trigger. Choose Create.
  • Name: Bonus Approval Trigger
  • Description: Bonus Approval Trigger
  • Please note that the identifier will be filled by itself

6. Create input for the process.

  • Open the Process Details by clicking on the Canvas.
  • Choose Variables. Then choose Configure to configure process inputs.
  • In the Configure Process Inputs window, choose Add Input to add parameters.

        Add the following parameter and click on Apply.

Name

Type

Bonus Context

bonus context

Process Input Fields are now visible in the Process Details.

7. Create and Configure Approval Form.

Tasks are a part of any business process. SAP Build Process Automation helps you to create forms that are made available to the business users in their inboxes to take relevant action.

You can create these different forms using the Form Builder embedded in the process builder using different form field options. You can design and modify them without any coding.

  • Go to process bonus_change_wf and choose + below the Trigger.
  • Choose Approval from the available options.

  • Choose Blank Approval.
  • In the Create Approval dialog box, do the following:
  • In the Name field enter: Bonus Approval Form
  • In the Description field enter: Form to approve or reject Travel.
  • Choose Create

  • Choose Open Editor of the Approval Form.
  • Design the Approval Form in the form builder by dragging-and-dropping fields into the form editor and configuring respective field settings.

Form Fields

Field Settings with Label

Headline 1

Bonus Request – Approval

Headline 2

Please approve or reject following request:

  • Add Input Fields, enter the labels and select the Read Only checkbox except comments text.

These fields will take value from the input of the process i.e., fields sent from the external system.

Form Fields

Field Settings with Label

Text

Employee Name

Text

Employee ID

Text

Bonus

Text

Release Status

Text

Target Revenue

Text

Comments

  • Save the Form.

Once the approval form is created its time to include it in the process flow and configure it to take the read only field values from process Input.

  • Go to process bonus_change_wf and choose + below the Trigger.
  • Choose Approval from the available options and select the approval form you created.

  • Select the Approval Form to configure the General information section.
  • Set the title of the task that will be displayed in the Inbox, enter the below details.

Fields

Values

Subject

Approve Bonus for employee employeename[Select employee name from Process Input]

Description

Bonus Approval

Priority

Medium

 

 

  • Set the Recipient, detail of the user who would receive the approval form in their inbox for approval.

Multiple valid email addresses can be added separated by commas in the below fields.

It is mandatory to fill one of the fields “users” or “groups”. We are filling “users” field here.

  • Similarly, go to the Inputs section and map the different input fields, which were marked as read-only in the approval form, by selecting the respective Process Content entry and save the process.

8. Create and Configure Action in the process flow.

Action is a feature in SAP Build Process Automation to connect processes with external systems, be it SAP or non-SAP systems.

In this blog, you will create an action project based on inbound service API (Here, we have created the inbound service URL for the custom business object in "Create Communication Arrangement for Custom Communication Scenario" section). You will use the PATCH call from SAP Build Process Automation action to the instance of the custom business object to notify if the bonus request has been Approved or Rejected on completion of workflow.

  • Create an Action Project.
  • Open SAP Build Lobby with your username and password, then do the following:

Under Connectors, select Actions.

  • Select ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model option from the list and select “CUSTBONUSPLAN” API which we have created

                

  • In the Create an Action project popup, do the following:
  • Enter the Project Name as Workflow Notification.
  • Enter the Description as Workflow Completion Notification.
  • Click Create.

As soon as the Bonus Notification Action gets created, SAP Build Actions will automatically open and the Add Actions to Bonus Notification pop up will appear.

In the YY1_BONUSPLAN entity, you want to only update the Release status field. Hence, in the popup, you have to select the PATCH method of /YY1_BONUSPLAN({SAP_UUID}) API and then click Add button.

  • As S/4HANA APIs need CSRF token, you need to enable it. Select Settings icon.

In the Project Settings window select CSRF and do the following:

    • Enable CSRF by selecting YES.
    • Under the Token Fetch End Point, enter: /

After that you can also test your action by navigating to “Test” Tab and provide the input values in the “Test Input Values” section and click the Test button. It should give “204” response and the test data should be reflected in the instance of custom business object.

  • Save the Action Project and Release the action Project.

You will now release the action project to create version(s) and then publish a selected version in the action repository. It is then these published actions that can be used in different processes and applications to connect to external systems.

  • To release a version of the action project, click Release from top-right corner.
  • Enter the Release Notes: “Bonus Completion Notification V1”

  • Publish Action Project.

Once the action project is released, you can then publish any release version of the action by clicking Publish to Library from top-right corner.

9. Add Action Project to Business Process.

Add the action in the business process to connect to send the workflow completion notification back to the system that triggered the workflow. You can do this by choosing the relevant action from the action’s library, and then configuring the action’s parameters.

  • Go to lobby and open project custombonusplan. Navigate to open the process builder canvas for bonus_change_wf and click on expand ‘>’ for the Bonus Approval form.
